response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack engineer with strong systems-language work in DLang (including low-latency, async I/O microservices at Dunnhumby) and a deep, decade-long geospatial background via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no Rust and no navigation/routing-tile experience. Worth a screening call to gauge how transferable the DLang low-latency work is and appetite to ramp into C++, but they are not an obvious fit.
strengths: Strong geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk)
Low-latency, async, microservices systems work in DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Solid Linux, craftsmanship and TDD background with willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience (~6 months in 2014–2015) and no Rust
No direct navigation systems, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node and AI tooling — drift away from systems programming
